source_pr.title: ddl:support add index rollback of partitioned table
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

* This PR solves the problem of  partitioned table add index does not support `rollback`.
### What is changed and how it works?
* Handle the rolling back job and finish the PR (support drop index for the partitioned table) #7306 first.
* `Drop index` mainly deletes `indexInfo`.
* If add index job rollbacks in write reorganization state, its need to delete all keys which has been added.
* Delete all keys is done in the `delete range`.
*  Fix issue #7223

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

PTAL @tiancaiamao @winkyao @zimulala .

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky around DDL/schema changes where schema propagation is asynchronous. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
